eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
emit: verb. To send out or give off.
emits: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of emit
emote: verb. To display or express (emotions, mental states, etc.) openly, particularly while acting, and especially in an excessive manner.
emotes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of emote
ism: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masimasi.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
meet: verb. To make contact (with someone) while in proximity.
mem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mangala.
memo: noun. A short note; a memorandum.
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
mete: verb. To dispense, measure in order to dispense, allot (especially punishment, reward etc.).
metis: noun. A person of mixed-race ancestry.
mim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alacatlatzala Mixtec.
mime: noun. A form of acting without words; pantomime.
mimeo: noun. Clipping of mimeograph (“type of copying machine”).
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
mise: noun. The issue in a writ of right.
mismeet: verb. To come together but fail to properly communicate or connect in positive ways.
mismet: verb. simple past and past participle of mismeet
miso: noun. A thick paste made by fermentation of soybeans with the mold Aspergillus oryzae, used in making soups and sauces.
mist: noun. Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.
mite: noun. Any of many minute arachnids which, along with the ticks, comprise subclass Acari (syn.
moist: adjective. Characterized by the presence of moisture; not dry; slightly wet; damp.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mome: noun. A dull or stupid person.
mos: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mooré.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
mote: noun. A small particle; a speck.
omit: verb. To leave out or exclude.
omits: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of omit
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seem: verb. To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
seme: noun. Anything which serves for any purpose as a substitute for an object of which it is, in some sense, a representation, sign, or symbol.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
some: pronoun. A certain number (multiple but not all).
sometime: adverb. At an indefinite but stated time in the past or future.
somite: noun. One of the paired masses of mesoderm distributed along the sides of the neural tube that will eventually become dermis, skeletal
sot: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sotho.
stem: noun. The stock of a family; a race or generation of progenitors.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teem: verb. To be stocked to overflowing.
teems: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of teem
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.

tommies: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tommy
